page_summary:
A scanned front page from the Northeast Star-Review (Los Angeles, California), dated Thursday, April 17, 1975. The clipping was sourced from Newspapers.com and downloaded on April 9, 2025. The PDF text layer contains only source metadata with no recoverable article text.

The April 1975 date places this during a significant period in Dick's career. Flow My Tears, the Policeman Said had been published in 1974 and would win the John W. Campbell Memorial Award that year. Dick was living in Southern California, actively writing the Exegesis, and engaged in his most sustained period of public appearances and essay writing. A front-page placement in a local Los Angeles community newspaper suggests a newsworthy story, possibly related to Dick or the literary and cultural life of his neighborhood.
